body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 

Hoists scalar parameters out of broadcast expressions to avoid that an entire (lazily evaluated) parameter struct becomes part of a broadcast tree when only a scalar is needed, putting pressure on parameter memory.

title: Hoist scalar parameters out of broadcast expressions

body:
After we made precipitation part of the working fluid in `Atmos`, we came up with a naming convention in `CloudMicrophysics`:

- `q_liq` means all liquid water and `q_ice` means all ice. (This is what you pass into `Thermodynamics`, because now everything is part of the working fluid. )

- `q_lcl` means cloud liquid and `q_icl` means cloud ice. (Names inherent to 1M microphysics scheme)

It follows that `q_liq = q_lcl + q_rai` and `q_ice = q_icl + q_sno` in 1M microphysics. This allows us to use consistent variable names between the 0M, 1M and P3 schemes. It also keeps the names short, in case we need to augment them with additional things like `_safe`, `sgs`, `level` etc.

This PR is the first step to updating ClimaAtmos to this convention. This will be the most painful step, because it is changing the names of prognostic variables in the 1M scheme. It's a potentially a breaking change in the Coupler and might affect the restart files and reproducibility tests. 

I think it's worth it though, because a consistent name convention will allow for easier debugging and code review. The main benefit is a clear and consistent distinction between a names that contain all liquid water and all ice (`q_liq` and `q_ice`) that should always be used when interacting with Thermodynamics, and microphysics sub-categories that may be different for different microphysics schemes.


In the next PR I will deal with changing `q_liq_rai` and `q_ice_sno` and add a consistent name for when microphysics tracers are clipped. There is some more cleanup to be done in cloud_fraction and physical_state in setups.

@haakon-e - lmk how much you want me to update the 2MP3 areas.
title: Part one of microphysics names cleanup

body: Part two of the rename: `q_liq_rai->q_liq` and  `q_ice_sno->q_ice`.
title: Part two of microphysics names cleanup
